transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: BusinessNetflix Will Acquire Warner Bros
SOURCE: Forbes

ARTICLE:

BreakingBusinessNetflix Will Acquire Warner Bros. In $83 Billion Deal—Discovery Will Be Split OffBySiladitya Ray,Forbes Staff. Siladitya Ray is a New Delhi-based Forbes news team reporter.Follow AuthorDec 05, 2025, 07:52am ESTDec 05, 2025, 08:53am ESTShareSaveCommentToplineNetflix on Friday announced it had reached a deal to acquire Warner Bros.— including the company's film and television studios and streamer HBO Max—for $82.7 billion, in move that follows a weeks-long bidding war in which the streaming giant beat out Paramount and Comcast.Warner Bros. Discovery has reportedly entered exclusive talks with Netflix for a sale of its studio and TV businesses.Anadolu via Getty ImagesKey FactsIn a press statement the company said the cash and stock deal is valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share and it will close after the media giant's TV network business is spun off into a separate public company, Discovery Global.The deal is subject to regulatory approvals and is expected to be completed in the third quarter of 2026.If it goes through, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash per share and $4.501 worth of shares of Netflix's stock.According to Deadline and CNN, Paramount Skydance had offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. Discovery business, including its cable channels like CNN and TNT. Comcast also made a bid only for Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming businesses.Bloomberg previously reported that Netflix is offering Warner Bros. Discovery a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators don't clear the deal.
---

```json
{
  "summary": "Netflix announced on December 5, 2025, that it has reached an agreement to acquire Warner Bros., including its film and television studios and streaming service HBO Max, for $82.7 billion. The deal, valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share, will close after WBD's TV network business is spun off into a separate public company called Discovery Global. Each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash per share and $4.50 worth of Netflix stock. The acquisition follows a competitive bidding process in which Netflix outbid Paramount and Comcast. Paramount Skydance reportedly offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. Discovery business, including cable channels like CNN and TNT, while Comcast bid only for the studio and streaming assets. The transaction is subject to regulatory approval and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026. Netflix has reportedly offered a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators reject the deal.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Netflix will acquire Warner Bros., including film/TV studios and HBO Max, for $82.7 billion at $27.75 per share",
    "Each Warner Bros. Discovery shareholder will receive $23.25 cash and $4.50 in Netflix stock per share",
    "WBD's TV network business will be spun off into a separate public company called Discovery Global before the deal closes",
    "Netflix outbid competitors Paramount Skydance ($27/share for entire WBD) and Comcast (bid for studio/streaming only)",
    "The deal requires regulatory approval and is expected to close in Q3 2026, with a reported $5 billion breakup fee if blocked"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Forbes staff reporter",
    "Netflix press statement",
    "Deadline",
    "CNN",
    "Bloomberg"
  ]
}
```

The deal, valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share, will close after WBD's TV network business is spun off into a separate public company called Discovery Global. Paramount Skydance reportedly offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. parsed_output.summary: Netflix announced on December 5, 2025, that it has reached an agreement to acquire Warner Bros., including its film and television studios and streaming service HBO Max, for $82.7 billion. The deal, valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share, will close after WBD's TV network business is spun off into a separate public company called Discovery Global. Each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash per share and $4.50 worth of Netflix stock. The acquisition follows a competitive bidding process in which Netflix outbid Paramount and Comcast. Paramount Skydance reportedly offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. Discovery business, including cable channels like CNN and TNT, while Comcast bid only for the studio and streaming assets. The transaction is subject to regulatory approval and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026. Netflix has reportedly offered a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators reject the deal.
key_facts: Netflix will acquire Warner Bros., including film/TV studios and HBO Max, for $82.7 billion at $27.75 per share
Each Warner Bros. Discovery shareholder will receive $23.25 cash and $4.50 in Netflix stock per share
WBD's TV network business will be spun off into a separate public company called Discovery Global before the deal closes
Netflix outbid competitors Paramount Skydance ($27/share for entire WBD) and Comcast (bid for studio/streaming only)
The deal requires regulatory approval and is expected to close in Q3 2026, with a reported $5 billion breakup fee if blocked
